relative_path: PERSONNEL/Lab Management team /Role descriptions /Role description Spatial profiling manager.docx
full_text:
Spatial profiling Lab manager at the Färkilä Lab

The main tasks of the Spatial manager are to manage, record, monitor and provide assistance to the Spatial profiling (tCycIF and GeoMX) operations and experiments in the Färkkilä Lab.

The specified tasks (% of working time allocation)

Management of resources (40%)

Reagents and antibodies

Ordering new antibodies and reagents

Keeping up the registries for all reagents, including antibody inventories and validation results. Adapting older registries to the new standards.

Prepare commonly used reagents and buffers for research use (PBS 1x, NaOH, Citric acid buffer, alcohol chain…)

Monitoring

use of antibodies/reagents

registries

Samples handling for t-CycIF,GeoMX,Xenium

FFPE blocks - keeping the registry up to date

Sections cut from the blocks

Keeping track of unused sections for upcoming validation experiments or future experiments

Assisting with slide barcoding

Organizing, storing and getting rid of the already utilized samples

Instruments

Managing the time allocation (schedules) of Lola and GeoMX

Making sure that the instruments are maintained properly

Keeping the connections to the manufacturers/IT

Troubleshooting if necessary

Ordering new parts for the machines if necessary

Revising registries of internal use and ensuring that SOPs are followed

Storage (Hard drives and P-drive)

Monitoring free space on Lola’s computer

Making sure that researchers have planned the storage of the data for the upcoming experiments

Monitoring free space on P-drive and updating the storage-responsible person

Management of experiments (40%)

Training new students (internal and external) on the use of reagents and instruments

Tracking experiments

Making sure new experiments are correctly planned and registered: See e

Then you can easily see enlisted all experiments registered:

tcycif form

Checking the availability resources (antibodies, instruments) together with the researchers who are planning a Spatial experiment

Monitoring experiments

the experiments and staining plans are recorded according to the SOPs

Naming of the files are correct according to the SOPs

Storage space is planned and used according to SOPs.

Updating the protocols and discussing with lab members periodically (every 3 months) about novel developments

Coordinating the required operations and ensuring the SOPs are followed also by external researchers

Experimental work (20%)

Running independently antibody and equipment validations (e.g. bleaching lights, spacers, novel tCycIF/pickSeq,GeoMx accessories)

Technical assisting in scientific experiments together with the researchers and collaborators

Performing and coordinating Xenium Experiments.

iLab

Lab coats

Critical functions
